There has been an increase Of
40 members in the chamber of
commerce in the last three months.


A Lucknow (Qndia) paper says
that delegates will be sent from
there to the Dry Farming congress
in Spokane.


E. A. Torrance, J. B. Snow and
wife and Fred A. Bennett are en-
gagged in three-cornered fight in
the superior court over the owner
ship of Standardization car.


John /. Wilson was here but a
little while yesterday until he
grasped that the chamber of com
merce and Mayor Pratt were boost
ing a Priest lake water supply.
Then of course John l.. crawled on
the Priest lake band wagon.


Pat Kennedy, the prospector
killed while on trip with Patsey
Clark and party to copper prop
erty in northern British Columbia,
was buried at Seattle. Kennedy
was well known around Spokane.
having followed railroad contract
ing for a number of years. The
Clark party has returned to Spo-
kane.


Work on the addition of three
stories to the Hutton building
making it seven, has begun, p. L.
Peterson is doing the work and
the cost will be $4,000.


Miss Ellen Daniels, 27o2 Gard
ner avenue, has gone to the Occur
d'Alene Indian reservation to fight
forest fires. She has a claim on
the reservation.


Samuel j. jackson in his divorce
complaint against Laura f. Jack
son said that he came home one
night rather Unexpectedly and
heard a gentleman visitor JUMP
through one of the windows and
found beer bottles on the floor.
Homer M. Daugherty, 20, &
woodsman, died yesterday at the
Deaconess hospital from injuries
received in logging camp neal
Elk. He was thought to have about
recovered when taken suddenly
| worse.


The contempt proceeding:
against Attorney Samuel L
Crane, for calling A. ]. Shaw, an
other lawyer, a liar" will be
heard Friday morning in Justice
Hyde's court.


George lIse, Howard stree
tailor. has been made defendant
in suit for $4,000 damages
brought by Joseph Y. Koch fol
alienationon of the affections o.
Mrs. Koch. Use and Koch at
tended lodge together, but it is
charged that Use would leave the
lodge room to Mrs. Koch while
her husband was still at the meet
ing.


Three box cars were reduced te
kindling wood in a switching
smashup on the Great Northern
tracks near the Union iron works


A woman applied to the prose


cutting attorneys office yesterday
| for a warrant for the arrest of G
| prominent merchant on a charge
that he was exercising hypnotic
| mowers over her. The warrant was
| refused. as the prosecuting attor
ney's office, with the approach OF
the primaries, is not borrowing any
more trouble.


r. Day sold lot 67x14o on Fiftf
avenue, between Browne and Di
vision, to Henry Phillips for $1,000
Three Italians, r. n. Perigion
Manaro Alberta, and ID. PetruZzl
were injured in blasting accident
in a construction camp near Che
ney yesterday. They were brought
to the Sacred Heart hospital
where Perigioni died early this
morning, and the others will prob
ably lose their sight.


In a struggle with an escaped
insane patient, Officer Deweese
was thrown over fence last night
on Lincoln street. The patient, f
